title: taraxacum gaelorum
intro: Taraxacum gaelorum, also known as the Scottish dandelion or Irish dandelion, is a plant species within the dandelion genus that is native to Ireland and Scotland. It is a perennial plant with a rosette of leaves and a yellow flower head, similar to the common dandelion. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Taraxacum gaelorum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves can also be eaten raw or cooked, offering various health benefits when consumed.
